questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
questions.answer: Not specified in the provided sources.
questions.gap: The statute does not specify any seasonal prohibition period for gas leaf blowers. Best practice suggests a year-round prohibition with exceptions for peak leaf-falling seasons (e.g., March 15–May 15 and October 1–December 31). This entity appears to have no such targeted gas leaf blower prohibition.

questions.question: What are the permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation?
questions.answer: Not specified in the provided sources.
questions.gap: The statute does not specify permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation, nor does it address seasonal restrictions. Best practice recommends weekday hours of 8:00 a.m.–8:00 p.m., Saturday hours of 9:00 a.m.–8:00 p.m., Sunday/holiday hours of 9:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m., and a prohibition during June 1–August 31.
